fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Indiana Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Indiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity indiana, how to transfer pharmacist license to indiana, pharmacist compact indiana, indiana pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Indiana Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: Indiana Professional Licensing Agency, 402 W Washington Street, Room W072, Indianapolis, Indiana 46204

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Indiana.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Register for a new account on MyLicense.IN.gov and complete the online application for Pharmacist licensure by examination or endorsement.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official Pharm.D. degree transcript to be sent directly from your accredited college or university to the Indiana Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided on MyLicense.IN.gov to complete the required criminal background check, which includes fingerprinting.

steps.title: Pass Required Examinations
steps.description: Register with NABP and successfully pass the North American Pharmacist Licensure Examination (NAPLEX) and the Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) for Indiana.

steps.title: Transfer Licensure (Endorsement Only)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, complete the Official Licensure Transfer Application through NABP, providing proof of active licensure in another state and good standing.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Indiana pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Indiana have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Indiana. To work in Indiana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Indiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Indiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Indiana
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in Indiana, you must submit your application and all supporting documentation through the MyLicense.IN.gov online portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Go to MyLicense.IN.gov.
2. Click 'Register a New Account' and follow the prompts.
3. Create your user ID and password.
4. Log in and select the option to apply for a new license.
